title: Kikaku Kaigi Project Meeting
wikitext:
[]: Kikaku Kaigi [Project Meeting]             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 2:18 am 企画会議 ［設定］ 人類が生まれる前に高度なテクノロジーをもつ先史文明が２度存在した。最初の文明（第一始祖民族）はエヴァを造ったが、それが原因で滅ぶ。次の文明（第2 始祖民族）はロンギヌスの槍を造ってエヴァの封じ込めに成功、後に何者かがエヴァを復活させたときの対抗策として、世界各地に使徒を眠らせた。 ［解説］ この段階ではエヴァは「人の造りしもの」ではなく、始祖民族が眠らせたものを復活させたようです。そして、使徒はあくまでも、復活したエヴァを破壊するための存在です。 なお、言うまでもありませんが、ナウシカ,、ナディアなどの庵野監督が関係した作品とも非常に似ております。 高度なテクノロジーを誇った過去の文明、というのは、ＳＦアニメの代表的な設定です。この段階では、後のエヴァが持つオリジナリティや複雑さを想像するのは難しいものがあります。

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 13, 2008 2:25 am Needs better translation than this:- Planning Meeting Settings Unborn human beings have advanced technology to present twice a prehistoric civilization. The first civilizations (founder of the first race) is made to Eva, but because of滅ぶ. Next civilization (founder of the second race) is to create and Longinus spear successful containment of Eva, Eva after the unidentified man was revived when his counterpart as a messenger眠らせたaround the world. [Explanation] Eva is this stage in the "servants of the people-building" rather than the founder of ethnic眠らせたto revive him. And are those messengers, in order to destroy the return of the presence of Eva. However, to say nothing, Nausicaa, such as Nadia Hermitage Director-related fields are very similar work. Past boasted of its advanced technology to civilization, that is, SF typical cartoon setting. At this stage, after Eva's originality and complexity is difficult to imagine. ==== 2020 upgrade Planning meeting [Configuration] Before the human race was born, there were two prehistoric civilizations with advanced technology. The first civilization (the first founding people) created Eva, which causes it to die. The next civilization (the 2nd founding ancestor) built a spear of Longinus and succeeded in containing Eva, and put apostles to sleep all over the world as a countermeasure when someone later revived Eva. [Explanation] At this stage, Eva seems to have revived what the founding nations had slept on, rather than "man made artifacts." And the apostle is the existence to destroy the revived Eva. Needless to say, it is also very similar to the works related to Director Anno, such as Nausicaa, and Nadia. The civilization of the past that boasted advanced technology is a typical setting for science fiction animation. At this stage it can be difficult to imagine the originality and complexity of the later Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 2:35 am This seems (from the crappy auto-translation) to fit in with what Reichu was saying about Yamashita talking in Sore wo Nasumono about Evangelion being created by an ancient society, and then the Evangelion ending up destroying society and such, and the Spear of Longinus being a "safety device" (as it still ended up being). She also said something about "two phases" in terms of human technology, which may be related to "advanced technology to present twice a prehistoric civilization".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 13, 2008 4:23 am Source / original context / etc.? Oh, and for those curious about the Spear of Longinus blurb in Sore o nasumono , here's the (partial) translation, which has its dodgy bits: Spear of Longinus: By the time humanity evolved, two prehistoric civilizations with extremely advanced technology had led existences on Earth. The first civilization created Eva, which was the cause of their destruction, and the next created the Spear of Longinus and successfully contained Eva. Afterwards, as a safeguard in the event that someone revived Eva, Angels were planted [placed into slumbers] to act as fully-automated safety devices, so to speak.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sat Sep 13, 2008 8:36 am Preliminary Meeting [Theme] Before the birth of the human race, there twice existed prehistoric civilizations with advanced technology. The first civilization (the First Ancestral Race) created the EVA, but because of this they were destroyed. The next civilization (the Second Ancestral Race) created the Spears of Longinus, thus successfully containing the EVA; afterwards, as a countermeasure to anyone reviving the EVA, they planted Angels all over the world. [Analysis] At this stage EVA are not to be considered “man-made”, but beings resurrected by the Ancestral Races. Hence, the Angels exist largely to destroy the EVA and remove traces of their presence in the case of their revival. However, it goes without saying that very similar themes are to be found in some of Director Anno’s works such as “Nausicaa of the Valley of the Wind” and “Nadia”. Ancient civilizations that boasted great technology are a typical theme found in Science Fiction anime. At this stage, it is difficult to imagine works with the same level or originality and complexity coming after EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 13, 2008 10:22 am You know Moonspeak? I knew I was missing something with the "Angel" bit, but Yamashita left out the 'around the world' part, which threw me off. Fixed. Whatever this is, it predates the Proposal, since Adam was introduced by that point and the Evas were definitely artificial beings created from "him". Proposal attributes the Angels' creation to the First Ancestral Race, as well; the Second seemed to have kicked the conceptual bucket by that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Sep 13, 2008 12:21 pm This sounds neat and all, but what the hell is it from?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 12:45 pm Oh, this is awesome, awesome, AWESOME! Except for the fact that I was developing a story that had a rather similar backplot... I suppose I need to watch Nadia now to make sure my idea isn't too similar to something that actually ended up getting made. The whole page is probably superstuffed full of interesting information. Said page is here . I found it by doing a Google search for Evangelion and アルカ.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 13, 2008 1:05 pm It's basically, from what I can tell, going over NGE's conceptual evolution and changes observed through its various incarnations. The bit you posted is the "Project Meeting". The next one is the "Proposal", with all the tidbits drawn from said document. (So, no, nothing new on Arka. Sorry. ) Then it goes onto the screenplays (presumably what's in the Evangelion Original books), to the storyboards, and so on. Seems like a pretty interesting overview, but I wish they cited their sources more explicitly. (Well, maybe they do somewhere. I'd have to look around.) Also, they have more pages on NGE's history.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 1:33 pm Reichu wrote: It's basically, from what I can tell, going over NGE's conceptual evolution and changes observed through its various incarnations.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 13, 2008 1:47 pm NAveryW wrote: This is exactly what I was hoping for. In the words of Joe the Creationist, "Woo-hoo!"

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Sep 13, 2008 2:10 pm Awesome find. It'll be interesting to see how Eva and the Angels evolved. Too bad I have no translation capabilities... :( Any idea on its credibility?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 2:31 pm Zaque wrote: Any idea on its credibility?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 8:38 pm This site looks like it has some very, very interesting stuff . 以前掲示板で、謎の文字は、企画段階で存在した古代遺跡アルカの流れを引きずっているのではないかと書きました。最初のプロットで存在していたアルカが無くなったために、あの古代文字自体も出番を失ったのではないかと。 しかし、のちに、あの文字は死海文書のものであることが絵コンテからわかり、別の資料からジオフロントの中心がアルカと呼ばれていたことがわかったため、アルカはなくなったのではなく名前が変わった（もしくは消えた）だけで、設定そのものはリリスの卵として残っていることがわかりました。 ということで、上述のような内容でまとめました。 もちろん、この文字がビデオ版１２巻以降で出現する可能性はあります。その時は設定がまた変更されているかもしれません。

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Sep 13, 2008 9:45 pm Congrats on the awesome (and I really mean awesome ) find, NAW

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Sep 13, 2008 11:29 pm Again, can someone please clarify for me exactly what this is. Please?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2008 1:09 am NAveryW wrote: If the way I'm reading the Google translation of this passage is correct

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 16, 2009 6:44 pm Bumping this since another thread mentioned this one. Does this site have a creditable source? I'm guessing it's from some informative book or some such thing about Eva, but I didn't notice anything on the Japanese with the site (unless I just mis-looked something). I could MAYBE give this a whack with translating some of this for the wiki, if I can find the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2009 8:10 pm I couldn't find a source of the "project meeting" tidbit either. Yamashita does reference it in Sore o nasumono, so the fansite isn't pulling it out of nowhere, but further confirmation would be nice. Some Googling might be in order to see where, if anywhere, this info was published (more comprehensively than Yamashita's mention). There isn't any immediate gain in translating the other stuff -- it's speculation and analysis of the sort we do. What would be worth translating are some of the things it cites, like passages from the screenplays (which I have the books for).

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Apr 16, 2009 11:37 pm so is this like a proposal proposal???????????
